    Default Notes / Terms
    📋
    Terms of Payment

    When enabled, standard international payment terms will be printed on every invoice as a formal T&C block.

    📋 Preview — Terms of Payment
    1. LPO Reference: This invoice is issued against a duly authorised Local Purchase Order (LPO). Payment will not be processed without a valid, written LPO reference approved by the procuring entity.
    2. Payment Terms: Payment is due within the number of days stated on the LPO or this invoice, from the date of invoice or delivery (whichever is later). Unless otherwise agreed in writing, net 30 days applies.
    3. Title & Risk: Title to goods supplied remains with the Supplier until full payment is received. Risk passes to the Buyer upon delivery and acceptance.
    4. Late Payment: Overdue balances shall attract interest at 2% per month (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, whichever is lower) calculated from the due date until the date of full payment.
    5. Acceptance: Acceptance of delivered goods or completed services by the Buyer constitutes agreement to pay this invoice in full as presented.
    6. Disputes: Any dispute regarding this invoice must be raised in writing within seven (7) calendar days of receipt. Failure to dispute within this period shall be deemed acceptance of the invoice.
    7. Taxes: All prices are exclusive of applicable taxes (including VAT/GST) unless explicitly stated otherwise. Any applicable taxes are the responsibility of the Buyer.
    8. Partial Delivery: Partial deliveries may be invoiced separately. Each such invoice is due and payable independently on its own payment terms.
    9. Governing Law: This invoice and any dispute arising from it shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction of the Supplier's registered address, unless otherwise agreed in the LPO contract.
    🏦
    Bank Accounts

    Add one or more bank accounts. On each invoice you can select which account to display for payment.

    🐙
    GitHub Sync
    Store invoice data in a GitHub repository — works even when hosted on GitHub Pages
    ⬤ Not connected
    How to set up:
    1. Create a public or private GitHub repo (e.g. visualtech-data)
    2. Generate a Personal Access Token at github.com/settings/tokens — enable repo scope
    3. Fill in the fields below and click Connect & Sync
    4. To host this app: push this HTML file to a repo and enable GitHub Pages (Settings → Pages → Deploy from branch)
    💡 After a Pull from GitHub: Click Restore Built-in Products to re-merge EVAC, Cinema & Home Automation items (IDs 121–170) into your library. Your custom products and invoices are never deleted.
